aliases: Veterans History Project of the Library of Congress American Folklife Center
Veterans History Project, Library of Congress
description: collection of firsthand remembrances of U.S. wartime veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans History Project of the Library of Congress American Folklife Center (commonly known as the Veterans History Project) was created by the United States Congress in 2000 to collect and preserve the firsthand remembrances of U.S. wartime veterans.

description: non-profit organizations that transport US military vets to see the memorials of the respective war(s) they fought
group of museums and research centers administered by the United States government
description_wiki: The Smithsonian Institution ( smith-SOH-nee-ən; or simply the Smithsonian) is a group of museums, education and research centers, created by the United States federal government "for the increase and diffusion of knowledge".

description: year
description_wiki: 1945 (MCMXLV) was a common year starting on Monday of the Gregorian calendar, the 1945th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 945th  year of the 2nd millennium, the 45th  year of the 20th century, and the  6th   year of the 1940s decade.

description: public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan
description_wiki: The University of Michigan (UMich, U-M, or Michigan) is a public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States.

description: county seat of Washtenaw County, Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Ann Arbor is a city in Washtenaw County, Michigan, United States, of which it is the county seat.

description: high school in Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: Lake View High School is a public four-year high school located in the Lake View neighborhood on the north side of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: city in and county seat of Wayne County, and largest city in State of Michigan, United States
description_wiki: Detroit (  dih-TROYT, locally also  DEE-troyt) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Michigan.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Fort Sheridan may refer to:

description: seat of Pulaski County, and capital and largest city of Arkansas, United States
description_wiki: Little Rock is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Arkansas.

description: president of the United States from 1945 to 1953; politician (1884–1972)
description_wiki: Harry S. Truman  (May 8, 1884 – December 26, 1972) was the 33rd president of the United States, serving from 1945 to 1953.

description: type of military school
description_wiki: An officer candidate school (OCS) is a military school which trains civilians and enlisted personnel in order for them to gain a commission as officers in the armed forces of a country.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: forced transfer of American and Filipino prisoners of war by the Imperial Japanese Army in 1942
description_wiki: The Bataan Death March was the forcible transfer by the Imperial Japanese Army of around 72,000 to 78,000 Filipino (about 66,000) and American (about 12,000) prisoners of war (POWs) from the municipalities of Bagac and Mariveles on the Bataan Peninsula to Camp O'Donnell via San Fernando.

description: public holiday commemorating the surrender of Nazi Germany (8 May 1945)
description_wiki: Victory in Europe Day is the day celebrating the formal acceptance by the Allies of World War II of Nazi Germany's unconditional surrender of its armed forces on Tuesday, 8 May 1945; it marked the official surrender of all German military operations.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: During World War II, two operations in the Pacific theater were called Operation Coronet.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: special skills badge of the United States Army
description_wiki: The Expert Infantryman Badge (EIB) is a special skills badge of the United States Army.

description: Semi-automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M1 Garand or M1 rifle is a semi-automatic rifle that was the service rifle of the U.S. Army during World War II and the Korean War.
